segments.text: After these things, Jesus revealed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias. He revealed himself this way.
Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus, [^44] Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples were together.
Simon Peter said to them,
I’m going fishing.
segments.speaker: Simon Peter

segments.text: They told him,
We are also coming with you.
segments.speaker: Simon Peter

segments.text: They immediately went out and entered into the boat. That night, they caught nothing.
But when day had already come, Jesus stood on the beach; yet the disciples didn’t know that it was Jesus.
Jesus therefore said to them,
Children, have you anything to eat?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They answered him,
No.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to them,
Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They cast it therefore, and now they weren’t able to draw it in for the multitude of fish.
That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter,
It’s the Lord!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself (for he was naked), and threw himself into the sea.
But the other disciples came in the little boat (for they were not far from the land, but about two hundred cubits[^45] away), dragging the net full of fish.
So when they got out on the land, they saw a fire of coals there, with fish and bread laid on it.
Jesus said to them,
Bring some of the fish which you have just caught.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land, full of one hundred fifty-three great fish. Even though there were so many, the net wasn’t torn.
Jesus said to them,
Come and eat breakfast!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: None of the disciples dared inquire of him,
Who are you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: knowing that it was the Lord.
Then Jesus came and took the bread, gave it to them, and the fish likewise.
This is now the third time that Jesus was revealed to his disciples after he had risen from the dead.
So when they had eaten their breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter,
Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me more than these?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to him,
Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: He said to him,
Feed my lambs.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to him again a second time,
Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to him,
Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: He said to him,
Tend my sheep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He said to him the third time,
Simon, son of Jonah, do you have affection for me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Peter was grieved because he asked him the third time,
Do you have affection for me?
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: He said to him,
Lord, you know everything. You know that I have affection for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
Feed my sheep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Most certainly I tell you, when you were young, you dressed yourself and walked where you wanted to. But when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will dress you and carry you where you don’t want to go.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now he said this, signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. When he had said this, he said to him,
Follow me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Peter, turning around, saw a disciple following. This was the disciple whom Jesus loved, the one who had also leaned on Jesus’ chest at the supper and asked,
Lord, who is going to betray you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Peter, seeing him, said to Jesus,
Lord, what about this man?
segments.speaker: Peter, seeing him,

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you? You follow me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This saying therefore went out among the brothers[^46] that this disciple wouldn’t die. Yet Jesus didn’t say to him that he wouldn’t die, but,
If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is the disciple who testifies about these things, and wrote these things. We know that his witness is true.
There are also many other things which Jesus did, which if they would all be written, I suppose that even the world itself wouldn’t have room for the books that would be written.
